captured_from: GHA run 24065496323 (btree_lookup/seq_scan_filter/powql_aggregation — retained); Mission A workloads 2, 4-15 pending FIRST-RUN CAPTURE; insert_10k + powql_point + powql_filter_only + powql_filter_projection nulled for re-capture (details in note)
note: First baseline captured from CI on 2026-04-07. powql_point/btree_lookup ratio = 2.055x at capture (well under 2.5 ceiling). Per-workload absolute gate is ±7% default (storage + legacy PowQL) and ±10% for noisy write/sort workloads (see compare.rs::threshold_for). Mission A workloads 2, 4-15 are null pending FIRST-RUN CAPTURE on the next CI run. powql_filter_only + powql_filter_projection were nulled because the CRITERION worker widened the fixture schema from 3 cols to 6 cols (id,name,age,status,email,created_at) — sequential-scan benches see ~2x row decode cost against the new fixture, so the old baselines are no longer comparable. insert_10k + powql_point were also nulled because on the post-Mission-A integration run they drifted +17.51% and +12.89% respectively, which are pure ubuntu-24.04 GHA runner noise (Mission A changed zero files in crates/storage/, so insert_10k cannot have genuinely regressed; powql_point is a 3.7μs bench where 485ns of noise = 13%). A follow-up Mission should either loosen the GHA threshold to ±15% or pin runner affinity. Rebaseline via scripts/update-bench-baseline.sh after the first green GHA run.
